title: Licania michauxii
taxonomy: Licania michauxii is classified in the family Sapindaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry family. This family includes around 130 genera and approximately 1,500 species. Licania michauxii is further classified in the genus Licania, which comprises around 100 species of trees and shrubs. The genus Licania is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas, Africa, and Asia. The classification of Licania michauxii reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Sapindaceae family.

intro: Licania michauxii, also known as the Swamp Beech or Black Ironwood, is a small tree or shrub native to the southeastern United States. The bark and roots of Licania michauxii have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
